name: Amazon SES API
what_it_does: Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) — high-volume transactional and bulk email sending via AWS with SMTP and REST API interfaces, pay-per-use pricing starting at $0.10/1000 emails, and deep AWS ecosystem integration.
use_cases: Agents sending transactional emails (receipts, alerts, notifications) at scale using AWS-native infrastructure
Bulk email campaigns — agents sending marketing emails through SES for cost efficiency at high volume
AWS-native workflows — agents in Lambda or ECS sending emails without external service dependencies
Email authentication setup — agents configuring DKIM, SPF, and DMARC via SES for domain reputation management
Bounce and complaint handling — agents processing SES SNS notifications to maintain sender reputation and list hygiene
not_for: Teams without AWS expertise — SES setup (domain verification, DKIM, sandbox exit) requires AWS knowledge
Visual email builders — SES is API/SMTP only; use Mailchimp or SendGrid for drag-and-drop template builders
Quick no-config email sending — Postmark or Resend provide simpler onboarding without AWS account setup
best_when: You're already on AWS and need cost-effective, high-volume email sending without the overhead of a dedicated email service provider.
avoid_when: You need quick setup or advanced email marketing features — Postmark or Resend are far simpler for transactional email without AWS.

auth.notes: AWS IAM credentials (Access Key + Secret Key) with SigV4 signing. IAM policies control granular permissions per SES action (ses:SendEmail, ses:CreateTemplate, etc.). IAM roles preferred over access keys for EC2/Lambda deployments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 62,000 emails/month when sent from EC2; $0.10/1000 emails from other sources
pricing.paid_tiers: $0.10 per 1,000 emails; $0.12 per 1,000 dedicated IP usage; additional charges for attachments
pricing.notes: Among the cheapest email APIs at scale. EC2/Lambda free tier is 62k/month. No monthly minimums. Dedicated IPs add $24.95/month per IP.

performance.rate_limits: Default: 14 sends/second; burst to 200/s; daily send quota varies by account (starts at 200/day in sandbox)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS SDK provides comprehensive error types. SES-specific errors include MessageRejected, MailFromDomainNotVerified, SendingPausedException. Error codes are well-documented. CloudWatch integration for monitoring.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SES v2 supports ClientToken for idempotent send requests. Same ClientToken within 10 minutes returns same response without resending. SES v1 API does not have idempotency keys.
known_agent_gotchas: SES starts in sandbox mode — agents can only send to verified email addresses until sandbox exit is requested
Domain verification and DKIM setup required before sending — multi-step setup not suitable for instant use
Rate limits start very low (14/s, 200/day in sandbox) — request limit increases before building agent workflows
Bounce/complaint handling requires SNS topic setup — agents must configure SNS notifications to maintain list hygiene
SES v1 and v2 APIs have different endpoints and features — prefer SESv2 for new integrations (idempotency, templates)

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA BAA, PCI-DSS. AWS IAM with granular per-action permissions. SigV4 signing prevents replay attacks. IAM roles eliminate long-lived credentials. Multi-region data residency. Enterprise-grade AWS security posture.
